title: ShinyShift – Shiny Exchange Gem
description: Adds a hardcore ways to Shiny a Pokemon in Cobblemon
body:
# ✨ ShinyShift – Shiny Altar System for Cobblemon

ShinyShift introduces a structured, progression-based shiny system designed for Cobblemon survival and multiplayer environments.

The mod transforms shiny conversion into a deliberate altar ritual rather than a simple item interaction.

Shiny Pokémon remain rare, meaningful, and server-balanced.

---

## 🎥 Video Guide

Full tutorial:

<iframe width="560" height="315" src="https://www.youtube-nocookie.com/embed/6cHiikrDe0s" title="YouTube video player" frameborder="0" allow="accelerometer; autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; gyroscope; picture-in-picture; web-share" allowfullscreen></iframe>

---

# 💎 Core Mechanic – Shiny Altar Ritual

The old direct-use mechanic has been removed.

Players must now:

1. Build the Shiny Altar structure  
2. Place the required blocks correctly  
3. Position the Pokémon inside the altar area  
4. Hold the Shiny Exchange Gem  
5. Right-click to activate  

---

## 🏗 Shiny Altar Structure

The altar must be constructed exactly as designed.

It contains:

- Shiny Stone Block (center zone)  
- Thunder Stone Block (activation layer)  
- Multi-layer structure requirement  

### 📷 Structure Preview
![Layout0](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/f168afb0e4f8d84a783e18964c4ef82c969ffd55.png)
![Layou1234](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/842ba08095f9834081ac16e610f6fb63b1347bab.png)
![Layout5](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/104fd3a991960ff2ef9410f1098ddc0bb7786683.png)

Players must follow the structure layout precisely for activation to work.

---

## ⚙ Activation Rules

✔ Pokémon must stand inside the altar zone  
✔ Pokémon must stand on the **_Shiny Stone Block + Thunder Stone Block_** area  
✔ Player must hold the Shiny Exchange Gem  
✔ Right-click to trigger  

### Behavior

If the Pokémon is NOT shiny  
→ Converts to shiny  
→ Item is consumed  
→ Particles and sound effects play  

If the Pokémon is ALREADY shiny  
→ Nothing happens  
→ Item is not consumed  

If the Pokémon is recalled mid-process  
→ The gem is not consumed  
→ No unintended item loss  

---

# 🔥 Recipe & Balance Changes

- The Nether Star recipe has been removed in 2.0.0
- The Shulker Shell recipe has been removed in 2.0.0
- Designed for late game balance  
---

# 🧩 Current Crafting Recipe – Shiny Exchange Gem

The Shiny Exchange Gem now follows the updated altar-based progression design.

### Crafting Layout
![Shiny Altar](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/8be05bb782e97cbc15ba77cccbe9a3040315ccf8.png)

- Sea Lantern  
- Shiny Stone Block  
- Glowstone  
- Nether Star  
- Netherite Block  
- Glow Ink Sac  

### Structure Pattern

The recipe follows a layered pattern:

Top Layer  
Sea Lantern – Shiny Stone Block – Glowstone  

Middle Layer  
Glow Ink Sac – Netherite Block – Glow Ink Sac  

Bottom Layer  
Empty – Shiny Stone Block – Empty  

(Refer to the crafting preview image above.)

This recipe reflects the late game balance philosophy of ShinyShift.

# 🧪 Testing the Structure (For Builders & Server Owners)

For testing purposes, you can use a Structure Block to load the altar template directly.

You may:

- Obtain a Structure Block
- Load the altar template
- Preview bounding box
- Rotate if needed

Example reference images:

![structureblockgive](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/32851645515adc90b68f38b923d1449921601251_0.webp)

![shinyaltarstructureblock](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/46c979a6eb4f5ad05f641c4469a5f73a2a30f98a_0.webp)

![placeshinyaltar](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/61d4dbd3d2091e00fa55c398baac7f39e1442bf6_0.webp) 


This is intended for who want testing.

---

# ⚙ Compatibility

- Minecraft 1.21.1  
- Cobblemon 1.7.0 - 1.7.3 (1.6.x I don't sure) 
- Fabric & NeoForge supported  
- Java 21  

---

# 🌍 Localization

- English (en_us)  
- Vietnamese (vi_vn)  

---

# 📝 Developer Note

ShinyShift evolved from a simple exchange item into a structured shiny ritual system.

Feedback, suggestions, and bug reports are welcome.
